scoring_methodology.demand: 0-10 based on volume/intensity of user requests, complaints, and workarounds found across communities
scoring_methodology.competition: 0-10 where 10 means nobody addresses this gap and 0 means fully solved by existing tools
scoring_methodology.feasibility: 0-10 based on technical complexity, required resources, and time to market

ranked_gaps.name: Cross-Model Prompt Translation & Portability
ranked_gaps.description: Automated tool that translates and optimizes prompts when switching between LLM providers (GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama). Each model has different logic stacks and carefully tuned prompts break on model switches.

rationale.demand: Mass-market pain validated by TechCrunch, Tom's Guide, HN. Users actively switching between models and losing prompt quality. Every team using LLMs faces this.
rationale.competition: Zero dedicated tools exist. Anthropic's memory import is partial workaround for chat history, not prompt optimization. No tool auto-translates prompts across models.
rationale.feasibility: Technically achievable using LLMs themselves to translate prompts + evaluation frameworks to validate quality preservation. Could be built as SaaS or library.

ranked_gaps.name: Multi-Agent Prompt Optimization Platform
ranked_gaps.description: End-to-end platform for optimizing prompts across multi-agent systems including inter-agent communication, topology-aware optimization, and handoff prompt tuning.

rationale.demand: Active GitHub issues on kagent and strands-agents. Microsoft built Agent Lightning. MASS framework in research. Agent frameworks proliferating rapidly.
rationale.competition: Microsoft Agent Lightning exists but is research-grade. No productized SaaS solution. DSPy handles single-agent; Opik has basic agent support. Gap is wide.
rationale.feasibility: Complex but achievable. Requires deep integration with agent frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph). Build on top of existing optimizers like DSPy/GEPA.

ranked_gaps.name: Prompt Security Optimization & Hardening Platform
ranked_gaps.description: Dedicated platform that combines prompt optimization with security hardening: automatically identifies vulnerabilities, suggests prompt rewrites that resist jailbreaks/injection, and provides continuous security monitoring.

rationale.demand: HN Show HN for agent firewall. Research showing 100% evasion of guardrails. F5 launched enterprise product. Enterprise security is non-negotiable requirement.
rationale.competition: Promptfoo has red teaming but focuses on testing not optimization. F5 launched guardrails but not prompt optimization. No tool that optimizes prompts FOR security.
rationale.feasibility: Very feasible. Combine red-teaming (Promptfoo-style) with automated prompt rewriting. Security patterns are well-documented. Clear product path.

ranked_gaps.name: Unified Prompt Lifecycle Platform
ranked_gaps.description: Single platform covering full lifecycle: ideation -> authoring -> optimization -> testing -> deployment -> monitoring -> iteration, eliminating the need to stitch together 3-5 tools.

rationale.demand: Community identifies 19+ tools needed for full lifecycle. Feedback happens in Slack not tools. Teams frustrated stitching together fragmented ecosystem.
rationale.competition: Braintrust and LangSmith come closest but still miss automated optimization. No single tool does it all. Gap is narrowing but still significant.
rationale.feasibility: Ambitious but achievable incrementally. Key challenge is doing everything well rather than being mediocre at all stages. Better to start with 2-3 stages.

ranked_gaps.name: Cost-Aware Prompt Optimization Engine
ranked_gaps.description: Tool that automatically compresses/rewrites prompts to minimize token costs while maintaining quality. Includes cost estimation, cheaper model recommendation, and prompt efficiency scoring.

rationale.demand: Databricks reports 90x cost reduction possible. HN PromptOptimizer project. AdaptiQ saves 30% tokens. Cost is top concern for production LLM apps.
rationale.competition: Helicone caches responses. Some tools track costs. But no tool optimizes prompts specifically to reduce cost while maintaining quality.
rationale.feasibility: Highly feasible. Use smaller models to evaluate, compress prompts via LLMs, benchmark quality vs cost trade-offs. Clear metrics.

ranked_gaps.name: Domain-Specific Prompt Optimization (Healthcare/Legal/Finance)
ranked_gaps.description: Prompt optimization platform with built-in domain knowledge, compliance guardrails, and industry-specific evaluation metrics for regulated industries.

rationale.demand: Domain-specific LLMs proliferating (Med-PaLM, ChatLAW). Healthcare achieving 80-100% compliance accuracy with optimized prompts. Massive regulated industry spend.
rationale.competition: Zero domain-specific prompt optimization tools. Generic tools exist but lack domain knowledge and compliance awareness.
rationale.feasibility: Requires deep domain expertise, regulatory knowledge, and industry partnerships. High barrier to entry but also high moat. Start with one vertical.

ranked_gaps.name: Continuous Prompt Regression Detection
ranked_gaps.description: Automated system that continuously monitors prompt performance, detects regressions when models update or prompts change, and alerts teams before production impact.

rationale.demand: HN users report 'whack-a-mole' prompt editing. Prompts break on model updates. Critical production reliability concern.
rationale.competition: Promptfoo, Braintrust have some regression testing but manual/semi-automated. No continuous automated monitoring solution.
rationale.feasibility: Very feasible. Scheduled evaluation runs, drift detection algorithms, alerting integration. Can build on existing eval frameworks.

ranked_gaps.name: Framework-Agnostic Agent Prompt Optimizer
ranked_gaps.description: Prompt optimization tool that works seamlessly across all major agent frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, Smolagents, custom) via OpenTelemetry or universal hooks.

rationale.demand: 50+ agent frameworks exist. LangSmith locked to LangChain. Teams using multiple frameworks need universal optimization.
rationale.competition: Langfuse and Helicone are more agnostic via OpenTelemetry but lack optimization. future-agi/agent-opt attempts this but is early.
rationale.feasibility: Moderate complexity. OpenTelemetry standard helps. Need adapters for each framework. Community contributions can help.

ranked_gaps.name: Multimodal Prompt Optimization
ranked_gaps.description: Tool that optimizes prompts for multimodal LLMs including image, video, and audio inputs. Joint optimization of text and non-text prompt components.

rationale.demand: Research papers (MPO, UniAPO) demonstrate need. Multimodal models growing fast. But production use cases still emerging.
rationale.competition: No productized tool. Only research frameworks (MPO, UniAPO). PromptPerfect supports Midjourney but doesn't optimize multimodal inputs.
rationale.feasibility: Technically challenging. Visual token inflation, lack of process-level supervision. Requires significant R&D investment.

ranked_gaps.name: Open Source Platform + Premium Auto-Optimization
ranked_gaps.description: Open-source prompt management platform with optional premium automated optimization layer. Combines Langfuse-level platform features with DSPy-level optimization.

rationale.demand: Open source tools lack optimization. Optimization tools lack platform features. Teams want both. GEPA integration into MLflow shows direction.
rationale.competition: No tool combines open-source platform with automated optimization. Langfuse is closest platform, DSPy closest optimizer, but they don't integrate.
rationale.feasibility: Moderate. Could extend Langfuse or build new. Need both platform engineering and ML optimization expertise.

ranked_gaps.name: IDE-Native Prompt Engineering Extension
ranked_gaps.description: Deep VS Code / JetBrains extension that brings prompt editing, testing, optimization, and deployment directly into the IDE workflow.

rationale.demand: Multiple indie VS Code extensions exist (Prompt Runner, Prompty, PromptIDE) but none from major platforms. Developers want IDE-native workflow.
rationale.competition: Indie extensions exist but fragmented and limited. No major prompt platform offers IDE integration. Microsoft Prompty is closest.
rationale.feasibility: Highly feasible. VS Code extension API well-documented. Can integrate with existing platform APIs.

ranked_gaps.name: Prompt-as-Code GitOps Workflow
ranked_gaps.description: Native Git-based prompt management with branch/merge/PR review workflows, where prompts are first-class code artifacts with full CI/CD integration.

rationale.demand: VLDB paper on prompts as first-class citizens. Microsoft Prompty format. DevOps teams expect GitOps for all artifacts.
rationale.competition: PromptLayer has Git-like versioning. Promptfoo has YAML configs in Git. But no true GitOps workflow with PR reviews for prompts.
rationale.feasibility: Highly feasible. Git integration well-understood. Can build GitHub/GitLab apps. Clear product path.

ranked_gaps.name: Affordable Mid-Tier Pricing ($10-30/mo)
ranked_gaps.description: Prompt optimization platform with meaningful features at $10-30/mo for small teams and indie developers, bridging the gap between limited free tiers and $39-250/mo plans.

rationale.demand: 98% of small businesses use AI tools. Most prompt tools price them out. Langfuse Core at $29/mo is closest but limited.
rationale.competition: Some tools have free tiers. Langfuse Core is $29/mo. But meaningful mid-tier is rare. Most jump to $39-50/user/mo.
rationale.feasibility: Business model challenge more than technical. Need efficient infrastructure to serve at low price points.

ranked_gaps.name: Prompt Distillation for Edge/Mobile
ranked_gaps.description: Tool that compresses complex prompts into token-efficient versions optimized for edge devices and smaller models while preserving intent and quality.

rationale.demand: Edge AI growing but prompt distillation is niche. Most edge deployments use fine-tuned models rather than prompted ones.
rationale.competition: Zero productized tools. Only academic research. Wide open space.
rationale.feasibility: Technically challenging. Requires understanding of smaller model capabilities and prompt compression techniques.
